an1.21:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when undeveloped in this way, is so intractable as the mind [*citta*]. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped, is intractable.”
an1.21:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped, is intractable.”
an1.22:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when developed in this way, is so tractable as the mind [*citta*]. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed, is tractable.”
an1.22:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed, is tractable.”
an1.23:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when undeveloped in this way, leads to great harm as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped, leads to great harm.”
an1.23:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped, leads to great harm.”
an1.24:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when developed in this way, leads to great benefit as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed, leads to great benefit.”
an1.24:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed, leads to great benefit.”
an1.25:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when undeveloped in this way and not brought forth, leads to great harm as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not brought forth, leads to great harm.”
an1.25:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not brought forth, leads to great harm.”
an1.26:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when developed in this way and brought forth, leads to great benefit as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and brought forth, leads to great benefit.”
an1.26:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and brought forth, leads to great benefit.”
an1.27:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when undeveloped and not made abundant in this way, leads to great harm as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not made abundant, leads to great harm.”
an1.27:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not made abundant, leads to great harm.”
an1.28:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when developed and made abundant in this way, leads to great benefit as the mind [*citta*] does. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and made abundant, leads to great benefit.”
an1.28:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and made abundant, leads to great benefit.”
an1.29:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when undeveloped and not made abundant in this way, is conducive to suffering as the mind [*citta*] is. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not made abundant, is conducive to suffering.”
an1.29:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when undeveloped and not made abundant, is conducive to suffering.”
an1.30:1.1: “I do not see even a single other _dhamma_, bhikkhus, that, when developed and made abundant in this way, is conducive to happiness as the mind [*citta*] is. For, bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and made abundant, is conducive to happiness.”
an1.30:1.2: “Bhikkhus, the mind [*citta*], when developed and made abundant, is conducive to happiness.”
